items.briefing:
Australia's new SMS Sender ID Register is now live, meaning businesses must register their brand names to send SMS messages to Australians. This helps protect people from scammers impersonating trusted organisations like banks or government agencies — if a message claims to be from a registered brand but isn't, it should be blocked. Separately, a recent study found most organisations have experienced AI-related security incidents, a reminder to be cautious about what information is shared with AI tools at work.

Apple users should also be aware of a recently discovered vulnerability in the "Hide My Email" feature that could expose real email addresses. If you use this feature for privacy, check for any Apple software updates and apply them promptly. **Practical tip:** Always install device and app updates as soon as they're available — many updates fix security vulnerabilities before they can be exploited.

featured.title: SMS Sender ID Register goes live to help protect Australians from scams
featured.quote: SMS Sender ID Register launched by ACMA to combat widespread SMS scams affecting Australian consumers.
